description: Expand dataset catalog with source_type/modality columns, 6 new categories, 8 synthetic datasets, 5 curated datasets, source badges on public cards, and admin form updates.

userStories.title: Add source_type and modality columns to datasets table
userStories.description: As a developer, I need source_type and modality columns on the datasets table to classify datasets by provenance and data modality.
acceptanceCriteria: Create Supabase migration via MCP (mcp__supabase-db__apply_migration) adding: source_type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'collected', modality TEXT (nullable)
Add CHECK constraint on source_type: source_type IN ('collected', 'synthetic', 'curated')
No CHECK constraint on modality (keep flexible)
Existing rows automatically get source_type = 'collected' from DEFAULT
Verify migration applied: SELECT column_name FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'datasets' AND column_name IN ('source_type', 'modality') — returns 2 rows
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use mcp__supabase-db__apply_migration to run the migration. The migration SQL is: ALTER TABLE datasets ADD COLUMN source_type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'collected' CHECK (source_type IN ('collected', 'synthetic', 'curated')); ALTER TABLE datasets ADD COLUMN modality TEXT; Then verify with mcp__supabase-db__execute_sql.

userStories.title: Backfill existing datasets with modality and create new categories
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to set modality on all existing datasets and create 6 new categories for expanded data domains.
acceptanceCriteria: Run SQL to set modality on all 15 existing datasets: video_text for all video/evaluation datasets, image_text for Object & Face Identity Matching + AI Image Generation Evaluation + Product Image Annotation, mixed for Content Safety & Policy Review

Insert 6 new categories into dataset_categories: Multimodal Instruction Tuning (slug: multimodal-instruction-tuning, display_order: 9), Speech & Audio (slug: speech-audio, display_order: 10), Code & Math Reasoning (slug: code-math-reasoning, display_order: 11), Document Understanding (slug: document-understanding, display_order: 12), Video Understanding (slug: video-understanding, display_order: 13), Large-Scale Pretraining (slug: large-scale-pretraining, display_order: 14)
Verify: SELECT name, source_type, modality FROM datasets WHERE modality IS NULL — returns 0 rows
Verify: SELECT count(*) FROM dataset_categories — returns 15 (9 old + 6 new)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use mcp__supabase-db__execute_sql for all SQL. Modality mapping: Egocentric Activity Capture → video_text, Traffic & Transportation → video_text, Workplace Activities → video_text, Cinematic Action Footage → video_text, Henri Halle → video_text, Game Environment Capture → video_text, all Video Evaluation datasets → video_text, Object & Face Identity → image_text, AI Image Generation Eval → image_text, Product Image Annotation → image_text, Content Safety → mixed.

userStories.title: Seed Tier 1 synthetic datasets (batch 1 of 2)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to create the first 4 proprietary synthetic dataset entries.
acceptanceCriteria: Insert 4 datasets with source_type='synthetic', is_published=true. For each, look up the category_id by querying dataset_categories by slug first.

1) Multimodal Image Chat: category=multimodal-instruction-tuning, modality=image_text, total_samples=997000, type=instruction, slug=multimodal-image-chat, description='Multi-turn conversational data pairing images with instruction-following dialogues. Covers chart interpretation, financial documents, and general visual reasoning across multiple generation rounds.', annotation_types=['multi_turn_chat','instruction_following','visual_reasoning']

2) Multimodal Video Chat: category=multimodal-instruction-tuning, modality=video_text, total_samples=440000, type=instruction, slug=multimodal-video-chat, description='Multi-turn video conversation data for training models to reason about temporal sequences, actions, and events in video content.', annotation_types=['multi_turn_chat','video_reasoning','temporal_understanding']

3) Financial & Chart Reasoning: category=document-understanding, modality=image_text, total_samples=325000, type=instruction, slug=financial-chart-reasoning, description='Structured conversations around financial documents, data tables, and chart visualizations. Designed for training models to extract, interpret, and reason about quantitative visual data.', annotation_types=['chart_reasoning','table_extraction','financial_analysis']

4) Code Instruction Tuning: category=code-math-reasoning, modality=text_only, total_samples=3600000, type=instruction, slug=code-instruction-tuning, description='High-quality instruction-following data for code generation, comprehension, SQL queries, and programming tasks across multiple languages and difficulty levels.', annotation_types=['code_generation','code_comprehension','sql','instruction_following']
All 4 have total_duration_hours=0, is_published=true, source_type='synthetic'
Verify: SELECT name, source_type FROM datasets WHERE source_type='synthetic' — returns 4 rows
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use mcp__supabase-db__execute_sql. First query category IDs: SELECT id, slug FROM dataset_categories WHERE slug IN ('multimodal-instruction-tuning','document-understanding','code-math-reasoning'). Then INSERT with those IDs.

userStories.title: Seed Tier 1 synthetic datasets (batch 2 of 2)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to create the remaining 4 proprietary synthetic dataset entries.
acceptanceCriteria: Insert 4 more datasets with source_type='synthetic', is_published=true.

5) Math Visual Reasoning: category=code-math-reasoning, modality=image_text, total_samples=1700000, type=instruction, slug=math-visual-reasoning, description='Visual mathematics problems paired with step-by-step reasoning. Includes OCR-focused variants for extracting and solving equations from images of handwritten and printed mathematical content.', annotation_types=['math_reasoning','ocr','equation_solving']

6) Speech-to-Speech Training: category=speech-audio, modality=audio_audio, total_samples=3300000, type=generation, slug=speech-to-speech-training, description='Paired audio-to-audio training data for speech generation and voice synthesis models. Derived from long-form spoken content with diverse speaker characteristics.', annotation_types=['speech_synthesis','voice_conversion']

7) Text-to-Speech Training: category=speech-audio, modality=audio_text, total_samples=6500000, type=generation, slug=text-to-speech-training, description='Large-scale text-audio paired data for training text-to-speech and speech understanding models. Covers diverse reading styles, pacing, and content domains.', annotation_types=['text_to_speech','speech_understanding']

8) Synthetic OCR & Rendered Text: category=document-understanding, modality=image_text, total_samples=20000000, type=annotation, slug=synthetic-ocr-rendered-text, description='Millions of synthetically rendered text images with character-level and line-level annotations. Includes diverse fonts, rotations, lighting conditions, and background textures for robust OCR training.', annotation_types=['ocr','text_detection','character_recognition']
All 4 have total_duration_hours=0, is_published=true, source_type='synthetic'
Verify: SELECT name, source_type FROM datasets WHERE source_type='synthetic' — returns 8 rows total
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use mcp__supabase-db__execute_sql. Query category IDs first: SELECT id, slug FROM dataset_categories WHERE slug IN ('code-math-reasoning','speech-audio','document-understanding').

userStories.title: Seed Tier 2 curated datasets
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to create the 5 curated open-source dataset entries.
acceptanceCriteria: Insert 5 datasets with source_type='curated', is_published=true.

1) Video Captioning & QA Collection: category=video-understanding, modality=video_text, total_samples=70000000, type=captioning, slug=video-captioning-qa-collection, description='Comprehensive video-text collection spanning captioning, question answering, and temporal reasoning tasks across diverse video domains including instructional content, movies, and real-world activities.', annotation_types=['video_captioning','video_qa','temporal_reasoning'], tags=['source:PANDA-70M','source:ActivityNet','source:MSRVTT','source:MSVD','source:FunQA','source:CinePile','source:FineVideo']

2) Document & PDF Intelligence: category=document-understanding, modality=multiple_images_text, total_samples=25000000, type=annotation, slug=document-pdf-intelligence, description='Training-ready document understanding data covering PDF parsing, table extraction, infographic interpretation, and multi-page document reasoning. Processed into distributed-training-ready shards.', annotation_types=['document_parsing','table_extraction','pdf_ocr','infographic_qa'], tags=['source:CCPDF','source:PlotQA','source:ChartLlama','source:infographics-VQA','source:TAT-DQA']

3) Audio Understanding Collection: category=speech-audio, modality=audio_text, total_samples=2600000, type=captioning, slug=audio-understanding-collection, description='Curated audio-text pairs spanning environmental sound classification, music understanding, and speech captioning. Quality-filtered and formatted for multimodal model training.', annotation_types=['audio_captioning','sound_classification','music_understanding'], tags=['source:VGGSound','source:AudioCaps','source:Freesound','source:RAVDESS','source:MusicQA']

4) Interleaved Multimodal Documents: category=large-scale-pretraining, modality=multiple_images_text, total_samples=140000000, type=pretraining, slug=interleaved-multimodal-documents, description='Web-scale interleaved image-text documents preserving natural reading order. Designed for pretraining models that understand documents with mixed visual and textual content.', annotation_types=['interleaved_pretraining','document_understanding'], tags=['source:OBELICS','source:MINT-1T','source:MMC4']

5) Image-Text Pretraining Shards: category=large-scale-pretraining, modality=image_text, total_samples=500000000, type=pretraining, slug=image-text-pretraining-shards, description='Billions of image-text pairs processed into training-ready webdataset shards. Quality-filtered, deduplicated, and validated for large-scale multimodal pretraining pipelines.', annotation_types=['image_text_pretraining','contrastive_learning'], tags=['source:DataComp','source:COYO-700M','source:CC3M','source:CC12M','source:CapsFusion']
All 5 have total_duration_hours=0, is_published=true, source_type='curated'
Verify: SELECT name, source_type FROM datasets WHERE source_type='curated' — returns 5 rows
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use mcp__supabase-db__execute_sql. Query category IDs first: SELECT id, slug FROM dataset_categories WHERE slug IN ('video-understanding','document-understanding','speech-audio','large-scale-pretraining'). The tags array stores source dataset names for internal reference — these won't show on the public page.

userStories.title: Update PublicDataset type and public API
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the public API to return source_type and modality so the frontend can display badges.
acceptanceCriteria: Add source_type (string) and modality (string | null) to PublicDataset interface in src/types/data-catalog.ts
Update src/app/api/public/datasets/route.ts to include source_type and modality in the SELECT query string
Update the response mapping to include source_type and modality in the returned object
Verify: hit /api/public/datasets — every object has source_type and modality fields
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Read src/app/api/public/datasets/route.ts for the current SELECT and mapping. The select string needs source_type and modality added. The mapping object needs both new fields added. Read src/types/data-catalog.ts for the PublicDataset interface.

userStories.title: Add source_type badge to PublicDatasetCard
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to see whether a dataset is synthetic or curated so I understand its provenance.
acceptanceCriteria: Update src/app/components/catalog/PublicDatasetCard.tsx to show a source_type badge
Badge placement: between the category label and the dataset name
Badge logic: source_type==='collected' → no badge, source_type==='synthetic' → pill 'SYNTHETIC', source_type==='curated' → pill 'CURATED · TRAINING-READY'
Badge styling: font-mono text-[10px] uppercase tracking-wider px-2 py-0.5 rounded-full border border-[var(--accent-primary)]/20 text-[var(--accent-primary)]/70 bg-[var(--accent-primary)]/5 w-fit mb-1
Badge should not significantly increase card height — keep it compact (single line)
Cards with source_type='collected' look identical to before (no visual change)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill — check that synthetic datasets show SYNTHETIC pill, curated show CURATED pill, collected show no pill
userStories.notes: Read src/app/components/catalog/PublicDatasetCard.tsx for current card layout. The badge goes after the category <span> and before the <h3> name. Only render the badge div when source_type !== 'collected'. The PublicDataset type now has source_type from US-006.

userStories.title: Update admin DatasetForm with source_type and modality
userStories.description: As an admin, I need to set source_type and modality when creating or editing datasets.
acceptanceCriteria: Read src/app/admin/catalog/DatasetForm.tsx to understand existing form structure
Add source_type dropdown: options 'collected' (default), 'synthetic', 'curated'. Label: 'Source Type'. Styled like existing form selects.

Add modality dropdown: options (empty/null), 'image_text', 'video_text', 'audio_text', 'text_only', 'audio_audio', 'multiple_images_text', 'audio_video_text', 'mixed'. Label: 'Modality'. Optional field.
Place both fields after the existing type field in the form layout
Both fields populate from existing dataset data when editing
Both fields save correctly on create (POST /api/admin/catalog) and update (PATCH /api/admin/catalog/[id])

Update the admin API routes to accept and persist source_type and modality if not already handled — read src/app/api/admin/catalog/route.ts (POST) and src/app/api/admin/catalog/[id]/route.ts (PATCH) to check
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Read src/app/admin/catalog/DatasetForm.tsx for form structure. Read src/app/api/admin/catalog/route.ts POST handler and src/app/api/admin/catalog/[id]/route.ts PATCH handler to see which fields they accept. The PATCH handler likely builds an updateFields object — add source_type and modality to it. The POST handler may need similar updates.

userStories.title: Playwright MCP visual verification
userStories.description: As QA, I need to verify the expanded catalog renders correctly with new datasets and badges.
acceptanceCriteria: Navigate to localhost:3005/data-catalog
Scroll to BROWSE THE CATALOG section, take screenshot
Verify new category pills appear: Multimodal Instruction Tuning, Speech & Audio, Code & Math Reasoning, Document Understanding, Video Understanding, Large-Scale Pretraining
Click 'Multimodal Instruction Tuning' pill — verify 2 cards (Image Chat + Video Chat) with SYNTHETIC badge
Click 'Speech & Audio' pill — verify 3 cards
Click 'Large-Scale Pretraining' pill — verify 2 cards with CURATED · TRAINING-READY badge
Click 'All' pill — scroll through all datasets, verify lazy loading works
Verify collected datasets (e.g. Egocentric Activity Capture) show NO badge
Click Request Access on a synthetic dataset — verify Calendly modal opens
Resize to mobile (375px) — verify badges don't break layout, take screenshot
Check browser console for errors
Document any issues found and fix critical blockers
userStories.notes: Dev server on port 3005. Use Playwright MCP tools. The public API uses anon key (createClient without cookies) so all published datasets should be visible. There should be 25 total published datasets: 12 existing collected + 8 synthetic + 5 curated.
